abstract: In this letter, we propose a practical non-linear energy harvesting (EH) model and develop a corresponding resource allocation algorithm for simultaneous wireless information and power transfer (SWIPT) systems. Unlike traditional linear models that assume a fixed power conversion efficiency, our approach accounts for the physical limitations and saturation effects of real-world EH circuits. We formulate the resource allocation strategy as a non-convex optimization problem aimed at maximizing the total power harvested by energy receivers while guaranteeing minimum signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR) requirements for multiple information receivers. To solve this, we transform the sum-of-ratios objective function into an equivalent subtractive form, enabling an efficient iterative optimization process. Within each iteration, we employ semidefinite relaxation to solve a rank-constrained semidefinite program (SDP) optimally. Numerical results demonstrate that designing resource allocation based on this non-linear model yields significant performance gains over conventional linear models, which often suffer from resource mismatches in practical deployments.
